News Capstone » Notifications

Notifications news_app.notifications

Notification utilities triggered when an editor approves an article:

  1. send_article_email_to_subscribers() — sends an email to all readers subscribed to the article's publisher or author.
  2. post_article_to_x() — posts a short announcement to X (Twitter) via the X API v2.

Configuration: Email uses Django's standard settings (EMAIL_BACKEND, DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL). X integration requires X_API_URL and X_API_TOKEN environment variables — never hard-code credentials.

def get_article_subscribers(article: Article) → QuerySet

Return all readers who should be notified about the given article. A reader qualifies if subscribed to the article's publisher or the article's author. Uses distinct() to prevent duplicates.

Parameters:article (Article) — The article that was just approved.
Returns:QuerySet[CustomUser] — Distinct queryset of Reader-role users.
def send_article_email_to_subscribers(article: Article) → None

Send an email notification about a newly approved article. Composes an email with title, author, optional publisher, and full content, then delivers to every subscribed reader. Returns early if no subscribers have email addresses. Uses fail_silently=True to avoid crashing the approval request.

Parameters:article (Article) — The article that was just approved.
Returns:None
def post_article_to_x(article: Article) → None

Post a short announcement to X (Twitter) using the X API v2 with Bearer Token authentication. The tweet text is truncated at 280 characters. All exceptions are caught silently so a failure never prevents article approval.

Parameters:article (Article) — The article that was just approved.
Returns:None

Built with Sphinx 8.1.3 using the Read the Docs theme. News Capstone 1.0.0.
